key_insight: OrchRM learns a reward model for multi-agent orchestration using self-supervised win-lose pairs from intermediate artifacts, avoiding human labels and expensive sub-agent rollouts.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to train and scale LLM-based orchestrators more efficiently, with up to 10x lower token usage and up to 8% higher test-time accuracy.
method: Builds orchestration-level preference pairs from intermediate multi-agent execution artifacts
Trains a Bradley-Terry reward model without human annotations
Uses the learned reward to guide both orchestrator training and test-time scaling
evidence_signal: The abstract reports gains across mathematical reasoning, web-based question answering, and multi-hop reasoning, with up to 10x token savings and up to 8% accuracy improvement.
watch_item: Results depend on the quality of intermediate artifacts and the consistency of reward signals, so noisy multi-agent traces could limit transfer or stability.
